title: eriogonum ochrocephalum subsp. chrysops
intro: Eriogonum ochrocephalum subsp. chrysops, commonly known as Desert Sulphur Buckwheat or Chrysop, is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The medicinal properties of Chrysop also provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Chrysop can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its benefits.
